info.title: OpenCrane Control Plane API
info.description:
Multi-tenant AI agent platform management API.

**Authentication**

- *Human operators* — OIDC browser flow via `GET /auth/login` → `/auth/callback`. Session cookie is set server-side.
- *CLI operators* — Device authorization grant via `POST /auth/device`. The CLI opens the returned `verificationUri` in the operator's browser, polls `GET /auth/device/token`, and persists the issued token in `~/.config/opencrane/credentials.json`.
- *Automation / CI* — Bearer token via the `OPENCRANE_TOKEN` environment variable, validated against the `OPENCRANE_API_TOKEN` server-side env var.
- Endpoints tagged *Auth* and *Meta* (`/auth/*`, `/openapi.json`) require no credentials.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Absent when hasMore is false.

deviceCode.description: Secret code used by the CLI to poll for the token.

verificationUri.description: Relative URL the operator should open in a browser.

bearerAuth.description: Static bearer token. Pass as Authorization: Bearer <token>.

Return current auth mode and authenticated user identity (if any)
get.description: No authentication required. Returns 200 with the current session or an anonymous identity when no session is established.

mode.description: Active authentication mode for this instance.

post.summary: Exchange the current OIDC session for a short-lived token to the caller's OpenClaw pod
post.description: Single sign-on across the control plane and the tenant pod: requires an established OIDC session (cookie) and returns a short-lived, audience-bound token minted via the Kubernetes TokenRequest API for the caller's tenant. The token targets the OpenClaw pod's session audience (reachable at `ingressHost`) — it is NOT an `obot-gateway` token; Obot is called only from inside the pod. The tenant is resolved solely from the session's verified email, so a caller cannot obtain a token for another user's pod. Re-call before `expiresAt`; re-login only when the session itself expires. Returns 401 without a session, 403 when no tenant matches the session email, 409 when the pod has no ingress host yet or when the email maps to more than one tenant.

token.description: Short-lived bearer token bound to the OpenClaw pod session audience.

expiresAt.description: ISO-8601 expiry reported by the API server.

ingressHost.description: Host to reach the tenant's OpenClaw pod session API.

audience.description: Audience the token is bound to (the OpenClaw pod session, not obot-gateway).

403.description: Session has no email claim, or no tenant is provisioned for it.

409.description: The tenant pod has no ingress host yet.

get.summary: Redirect the browser to the configured OIDC identity provider to start login
get.description: Browser redirect — not intended for programmatic use. Returns 503 when OIDC is not configured.

parameters.name: returnTo
parameters.description: Path to redirect back to after a successful login.

get.summary: OIDC authorization callback — validates the response and establishes a session
get.description: Called by the identity provider after a successful login. Redirects back to the SPA.

post.description: Invalidates the server-side session. Does not perform IdP-side logout (RP-initiated logout is out of scope for Phase 5).

post.summary: Initiate a CLI device authorization grant
post.description: Returns a device code and short user code. The CLI prints the verificationUri for the operator to open in a browser. No credentials required.

get.summary: Activate a device grant in the browser (requires OIDC session)
get.description: The operator opens this URL after a CLI login prompt. If no OIDC session is present the user is redirected to the identity provider first. On success an access token is created and the CLI poll endpoint unblocks.

parameters.name: userCode
parameters.description: Short user code from the CLI prompt (e.g. ABCD-1234).

200.description: Grant activated. HTML confirmation page returned.

302.description: Redirect to OIDC login (no active session).

404.description: User code not found or expired.

get.summary: Poll for the access token after browser activation
get.description: Returns 202 while pending, 200 with token when authorized, 410 when the grant has expired. The token is delivered exactly once.

parameters.name: deviceCode
parameters.description: Secret device code returned by POST /auth/device.

410.description: Grant expired. Run `oc auth login` again.

get.summary: Retrieve the OpenAPI 3.1 specification for this API
